skill.name: managing-branches
skill.description: Investigates current Git branch state, creates branches from a chosen base, and reports branch-related issues.
skill.summary: Guide AI assistants through safe Git branch checks, creation, sync review, and conflict reporting.
skill.author: 1gy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell command examples, standard device redirection, and a weak-crypto blocker. Review found only bounded Git branch commands and a benign stderr redirect to /dev/null; the weak-crypto hit is a false positive on YAML description syntax. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or non-Git command execution.

low_findings.title: Bounded Git Command Execution
low_findings.description: The skill permits and documents Git commands for branch status, fetch, checkout, stash guidance, and conflict staging. This is appropriate for a branch-management skill, but it can change repository state and contact configured Git remotes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly allows Bash(git:*) and all command examples are Git operations. The commands are legitimate for the stated purpose, with residual risk from repository mutation and remote fetches.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography finding points to the YAML folded description marker, not to a cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata syntax for a multi-line description. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, hash operation, or encoded payload at that location.

low_findings.title: Benign Standard Device Redirection
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding is stderr redirection to /dev/null while counting commits ahead of the main branch. This suppresses command errors and does not read sensitive files or write repository content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only device-file access is a conventional shell redirection of stderr. It is attached to a Git query and has no apparent data access or exfiltration behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Commands Require User Awareness
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses external Git commands, including fetch and checkout operations. These commands are normal for branch management but should run only in repositories where the user expects branch and remote state changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command surface is visible and limited to Git. Risk remains operational rather than malicious because fetch and checkout can affect local branch state.

content.value_statement: Branch work often fails because the current state is unclear before changes begin. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through branch checks, creation, sync review, and issue reporting.
actual_capabilities: Checks the current Git branch and short working tree status.
Fetches remote branch information before reporting sync state.
Lists local branches with upstream tracking details.
Creates a new branch from a specified remote base branch.
Reports branch creation results, uncommitted changes, and encountered errors.
Guides basic conflict identification and staging after resolution.
limitations: It only documents Git command workflows and does not implement a separate Git client.
It does not resolve complex merge conflicts without user guidance.
It depends on the repository remote configuration and available Git credentials.
It assumes the user can identify the correct main or base branch.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Check the current repository state and create a new branch from the selected remote base.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Branch Sync Issues
use_cases.description: Review current branch tracking, remote sync state, and commits ahead of the main branch.

use_cases.title: Handle Branch Workflow Errors
use_cases.description: Respond to existing branches, uncommitted changes, fetch failures, and permission errors with clear next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Check Branch Status
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner status check before making changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the current Git branch, working tree status, upstream tracking, and whether this branch is ahead of the main branch.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a new branch from a known base branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new branch named feature/payment-copy from origin/main. Report the branch result and any issues before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Sync Problem
prompt_templates.scenario: Understand why a local branch is not aligned with its remote.
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate why my current branch is not synced with the remote. Fetch remote data, show tracking status, and summarize safe next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From Branch Error
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow triage after a failed branch operation.
prompt_templates.prompt: A branch checkout failed after I had uncommitted changes. Inspect the state, explain the safest options, and wait for my approval before changing anything.

output_examples.input: Check my current branch before I start work.
output_examples.output: Current branch: feature/login. Working tree has two modified files. The branch tracks origin/feature/login and is one commit ahead of main.

output_examples.input: Create a branch from origin/main for the settings page.
output_examples.output: Created branch feature/settings-page from origin/main. No uncommitted changes were present before creation. Remote fetch completed successfully.
Branch creation did not complete because a branch with that name already exists. Choose a new branch name or confirm that the existing branch should be used.

best_practices: Run a branch status check before creating or switching branches.
Confirm the intended base branch before creating a new branch.
Ask the user before stashing, committing, or resolving conflicts.
anti_patterns: Creating branches without checking for uncommitted changes first.
Assuming the default branch name without inspecting project guidance.
Resolving merge conflicts automatically when the correct change is unclear.

faq.question: Does this skill modify files?
faq.answer: It can guide Git commands that create branches or stage resolved files. It should report state before making changes.

faq.question: Can it work with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, if the local Git credentials and remote configuration already allow access.

faq.question: Does it push branches to a remote?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow fetches remotes and creates local branches, but it does not push.

faq.question: Can it resolve merge conflicts?
faq.answer: It can identify conflicts and guide staging after resolution. Complex conflict choices require user guidance.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code with Git command access.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The skill runs external Git commands. They are appropriate, but they can change branch state or contact configured remotes.
